rag.description: The RedoNavigation plugin navigates the browser forward in session history. It supports configurable repeat counts and optional delays between steps, enabling controlled multi-step forward navigation in RPA workflows and automated test suites.

qa.question: What does the RedoNavigation plugin do?
qa.answer: It navigates the browser forward in session history, equivalent to clicking the browser's forward button. The repeat count and inter-step delay are both configurable.

qa.question: What parameters does RedoNavigation support?
qa.answer: It supports Repeat (number of forward steps, default 1) and Delay (pause between steps in milliseconds or time format, default 0).

qa.question: How is RedoNavigation integrated into a Sequential Workflow?
qa.answer: It is used as an Action task node and maps to the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/forward endpoint.

qa.question: What is the recommended approach for multi-step forward navigation?
qa.answer: Pass `{{$ --Repeat:N --Delay:Ms}}` as the argument to control the number of steps and the pause between them in a single action.

qa.question: What delay formats does the Delay parameter accept?
qa.answer: Delay accepts a millisecond integer such as 1000 or a time span string such as 00:00:01. Values exceeding the maximum integer millisecond range are reset to zero with a warning.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for RedoNavigation?
What is the purpose of RedoNavigation according to its description?
qa.answer: It automates forward browser navigation with configurable repeat counts and delays to support multi-step RPA workflows and browser state management in automated tests.

qa.question: What happens when RedoNavigation is called with no forward history available?
qa.answer: The WebDriver forward call completes without error but has no visible effect if no forward history exists in the current session.

description: ### Purpose
The `RedoNavigation` plugin automates forward browser navigation, equivalent to clicking the browser's built-in forward button.
It provides a structured way to advance through session history with configurable repeat counts and optional delays between steps.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature             | Description                                                                                                                  |
| Dynamic Repeats     | Configures how many times the browser navigates forward in a single action invocation.                                       |
| Configurable Delays | Introduces a pause between each forward step, in milliseconds or time span format, to control navigation pace.               |
| Legacy Argument     | Accepts a plain integer directly as the repeat count for backwards-compatible usage.                                         |
| Delay Safeguard     | Logs a warning and resets the delay to zero when the configured value exceeds the maximum allowed integer millisecond range. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case             | Description                                                                                               |
| Multi-Step Traversal | Advances through a sequence of previously visited pages in a single action without manual forward clicks. |
| State Restoration    | Navigates forward to restore a known browser state after automated back navigation.                       |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                | Description                                                                                                    |
| Browser History Testing | Verifies that the browser correctly restores pages when navigating forward after a programmatic back sequence. |
| Navigation Flow Testing | Simulates realistic forward navigation patterns with controlled delays for performance test accuracy.          |

edge_cases: Already at the end of forward history — forward call completes without error but has no visible effect
Browser session ends before navigation completes — WebDriver exception propagates to the caller
Page load triggered by the forward step exceeds the implicit wait — downstream steps may interact with a stale or loading DOM
Single-page application updates URL state without a full reload — forward navigation behaviour may differ from a traditional page
annotations.expected_result: The browser navigates forward by one step in session history.
annotations.notes: When no argument or parameters are supplied the plugin navigates forward exactly once with no delay. This is equivalent to a single forward button click.

description: ### Default single forward navigation
Navigates the browser forward by one step using default settings.
No repeat count or delay is configured, so the action executes once immediately.

edge_cases: Argument value is negative — treated as zero; no navigation steps are executed
Argument value is zero — no navigation steps are executed
Forward history has fewer pages than the requested repeat count — remaining steps have no effect but complete without error
Page load between repeats takes longer than expected — the next step may act on a still-loading page
annotations.expected_result: The browser navigates forward three times in succession with no delay between steps.
annotations.notes: Passing a plain integer as the argument is the legacy shorthand for the Repeat count. It is equivalent to `{{$ --Repeat:3}}` but requires no parameter expression syntax. The Delay parameter also accepts a time span string such as `00:00:01`.

description: ### Forward navigation using an integer argument
Navigates the browser forward three times by passing `3` as a plain integer argument.
The integer is interpreted directly as the repeat count without requiring parameter expression syntax.

edge_cases: Delay is zero but Repeat is greater than one — steps execute as fast as the browser allows with no pause
Delay value exceeds int.MaxValue milliseconds — a warning is logged and the delay resets to zero
Forward history is exhausted before all repeats complete — remaining steps have no visible effect but do not throw
Network latency causes page loads to exceed the configured delay — the next forward step may begin before the current page finishes loading
Time format string is malformed — ConvertToTimeSpan returns zero and the delay is effectively disabled
annotations.expected_result: The browser navigates forward three times, pausing 1000 milliseconds between each step.
annotations.notes: The `{{$ --Repeat:3 --Delay:1000}}` expression sets both the repeat count and the inter-step delay in milliseconds. The Delay parameter also accepts a time span string such as `00:00:01` for the same one-second pause.

description: ### Forward navigation with repeat count and delay
Navigates the browser forward three times, pausing 1000 milliseconds between each step.
The `{{$ --Repeat:3 --Delay:1000}}` argument expression sets both the repeat count and the inter-step delay in a single value.
Controls the pause between each forward navigation step.
Accepts a value in milliseconds or a time span string such as `00:00:01` for one second.
When the configured delay exceeds the maximum allowed integer millisecond value, a warning is logged and the delay resets to zero.
parameters.name: Delay

description: Sets how many times the browser navigates forward in one action invocation.
Useful when advancing through multiple pages is required without issuing separate forward commands.
When no valid value is provided, the browser navigates forward exactly once.
parameters.name: Repeat

description: Provides the primary input for the forward navigation action.
Accepts either a plain integer as the repeat count or a parameter expression such as `{{$ --Repeat:3 --Delay:1000}}` for combined repeat and delay control.
When a plain integer is supplied and no `Repeat` parameter is present, the integer is used directly as the repeat count.
properties.name: Argument

summary: RedoNavigation navigates the browser forward in session history, equivalent to clicking the browser's built-in forward button.
It supports configurable repeat counts and optional delays between steps, enabling controlled multi-step forward navigation in automation workflows.
Common uses include advancing through previously visited pages in RPA flows and managing browser state during end-to-end testing.
